replies.msg:
TDOC is stopping at the 38.2% retrace of the drawdown ($206). This uptrend is either corrective (B wave) or we are dealing with a leading diagonal on the first wave of a new uptrend. On a micro level, there is a moderately clear 5-wave move up, which has me leaning towards the latter. If price breaks back below $192-$190 on any retrace from here, the odds of this uptrend being a b wave goes up. a break to new lows confirms it.

This is why I always start small. The C wave from here points towards the $160 level (about a 25% drawdown from our current entry, which I can take as a long-term investor on a small portion of our portfolio). If we retrace to the 61.8% retrace level, it points to the $171 region that we already identified.

On the flip side, if we correct to the $192 level, bottom, and move higher, it will be likely the bottom is in for TDOC on this selloff.

Pompeo發出最後警告

U.S. says China 'bullying' UK, cites HSBC Hong Kong-related accounts
https://www.reuters.com/article/us-hongkong-security-usa-hsbc/u-s-dismayed-by-hsbcs-reported-actions-over-hong-kong-accounts-statement-idUSKBN25M1DT

WASHINGTON/LONDON (Reuters) - U.S. Secretary of State Mike Pompeo on Wednesday renewed criticism of British Bank HSBC (HSBA.L) for its reported treatment of pro-democracy customers in Hong Kong, saying China was “bullying” the United Kingdom.

FILE PHOTO: HSBC logo is seen in the financial district in New York, U.S., August 7, 2019. REUTERS/Brendan McDermid/File Photo
Pompeo cited reports of Hong Kong-based executives at Next Media being unable to access their HSBC bank accounts and said the bank was “maintaining accounts for individuals who have been sanctioned for denying freedom for Hong Kongers, while shutting accounts for those seeking freedom.”

“Free nations must ensure that corporate interests are not suborned by the CCP (Chinese Communist Party) to aid its political repression,” Pompeo said in a statement.

“We stand ready to help the British government and its companies resist CCP bullying and stand for freedom.”

Representatives for the London-based bank, in an email, declined to comment.

Britain’s Foreign & Commonwealth Office did not immediately respond to a request for comment on Pompeo’s assertion that China was bullying the UK.

HSBC has in recent months faced mounting pressure on both sides of the Atlantic, as it attempts to balance its need to maintain access to the Chinese market with appeasing lawmakers in the United States and Britain critical of Beijing’s handling of the democracy movement in Hong Kong.

Last month, British Foreign Secretary Dominic Raab reprimanded HSBC and other banks for supporting China’s new security law, saying the rights of the people of Hong Kong should not be sacrificed for bankers’ bonuses.

Senior British and U.S. politicians criticized HSBC and Standard Chartered (STAN.L) in June after the banks backed China’s national security law for the territory.

Washington has criticized Beijing’s crackdown against pro-democracy opposition in the now Chinese-ruled city following a sweeping new security law imposed on Hong Kong on June 30 that was widely condemned by Western nations.

Hong Kong media tycoon Jimmy Lai, a prominent democracy activist and top executive at Next Digital, was arrested Aug. 10 under the new law, further stoking concerns about media and other freedoms
promised to Hong Kong when it returned to China in 1997.

美國駐華大使布蘭斯塔德（Terry Branstad）卸任，已在返回美國途中。美國務卿蓬佩奧（Mike Pompeo）9月14日在Twitter發文，感謝布蘭斯塔德3年以來的貢獻。

美國哥倫比亞廣播公司（CBS）14日報道，華府撤換駐中國大使布蘭斯塔德。

蓬佩奧沒說明布蘭斯塔德被撤換的原因，也未有說美國總統特朗普（Donald Trump）將提名誰接任。

布蘭斯塔德以8月以《基於對等重置關係》（Resetting the Relationship Based on Reciprocity）為題發表署名文章，向《人民日報》投稿而遭拒絶。蓬佩奧予以指摘，《人民日報》發言人亦指「內容漏洞百出，與事實嚴重不符」，因此拒絶刊發。

《人民日報》稱「長期以來，對於包括布蘭斯塔德大使在內的、對中國秉持客觀公正態度的各國友人，向《人民日報》投稿並申請刊發文章一事，我們一直持積極開放的態度。」

蓬佩奧9日在一份題為《中國虛偽的宣傳系統》的英文聲明稱：「《人民日報》的回應再次暴露中國共產黨對言論自由以及認真的智力辯論的恐懼——還有北京抱怨其他國家對它缺乏公正和互惠的對待時的虛偽。」

但開風氣不為師，不論我們的勒索成功與否，希望一眾尊貴的議員以及從政的同路人會以我們的決定作為警惕，切忌留戀光環，在必要時用盡任何方式繼續為香港民族自由而犧牲的手足抗爭下去。

I am Nobody,
